017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.observation;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
021
022import java.time.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.metrics.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.tracing.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.tracing.Scope;
027
028/**
029 * Factory for custom (non-request) {@link Observation}s, composing a {@link MetricsRecorder} and a
030 * {@link TracerHook}.
031 *
032 * <p>
033 * This is the explicit programmatic observation substrate for {@code juneau-rest-server}: it reuses the
034 * exact same two SPIs the request path uses ({@link MetricsRecorder}, {@link TracerHook}) and the same
035 * {@code metricName} / {@code metricTags} comma-separated tag model, but is decoupled from
036 * {@code RestRequest} so application code can instrument an arbitrary block of work. Juneau deliberately
037 * has no method-interception / AOP substrate, so observation is explicit (a try-with-resources block)
038 * rather than annotation-driven &mdash; consistent with the framework's explicit-over-magic philosophy.
039 *
040 * <h5 class='topic'>No-backend contract</h5>
041 *
042 * <p>
043 * An {@code Observer} built from the no-op SPIs ({@link #NOOP}) never takes a timestamp and hands back
044 * {@link Observation#NOOP}, so an observation in an unconfigured process costs nothing beyond the
045 * try-with-resources scaffolding. {@link #start(String, String)} short-circuits to the no-op handle
046 * whenever <i>both</i> backends are the no-op singletons.
047 *
048 * <h5 class='topic'>Wiring</h5>
049 *
050 * <p>
051 * Typically obtained from a {@code RestContext}'s bean store &mdash; register the same
052 * {@code @Bean MetricsRecorder} / {@code @Bean TracerHook} you already register for request-path
053 * observability, then build an {@code Observer} from them (or install one as the {@link Observations}
054 * default). Construct directly for tests:
055 *
056 * <p class='bjava'>
057 *    Observer <jv>o</jv> = <jk>new</jk> Observer(<jk>new</jk> MicrometerMetricsRecorder(<jv>registry</jv>), <jk>new</jk> OtelTracerHook(<jv>otel</jv>));
058 * </p>
059 *
060 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
061 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Observations}
062 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Observation}
063 *    <li class='jc'>{@link MetricsRecorder}
064 *    <li class='jc'>{@link TracerHook}
065 * </ul>
066 *
067 * @since 10.0.0
068 */
069@SuppressWarnings({
070   "resource" // start(...) returns the Observation for the caller's try-with-resources; ActiveObservation holds the tracer Scope it closes in its own close().
071})
072public final class Observer {
073
074   /**
075    * Observer backed by the no-op metrics recorder and tracer &mdash; produces only
076    * {@link Observation#NOOP}.
077    */
078   public static final Observer NOOP = new Observer(NoOpMetricsRecorder.INSTANCE, NoOpTracerHook.INSTANCE);
079
080   // Constant name intentionally uses camelCase to match observation/metrics naming conventions.
081   @SuppressWarnings("java:S115")
082   private static final String ARG_recorder = "recorder";
083   // Constant name intentionally uses camelCase to match observation/metrics naming conventions.
084   @SuppressWarnings("java:S115")
085   private static final String ARG_tracer = "tracer";
086   // Constant name intentionally uses camelCase to match observation/metrics naming conventions.
087   @SuppressWarnings("java:S115")
088   private static final String ARG_name = "name";
089
090   private final MetricsRecorder recorder;
091   private final TracerHook tracer;
092   private final boolean active;
093
094   /**
095    * Constructor.
096    *
097    * @param recorder The metrics recorder to time observations against. Must not be <jk>null</jk>;
098    *    use {@link NoOpMetricsRecorder#INSTANCE} to disable metrics.
099    * @param tracer The tracer to open spans against. Must not be <jk>null</jk>; use
100    *    {@link NoOpTracerHook#INSTANCE} to disable tracing.
101    */
102   public Observer(MetricsRecorder recorder, TracerHook tracer) {
103      this.recorder = assertArgNotNull(ARG_recorder, recorder);
104      this.tracer = assertArgNotNull(ARG_tracer, tracer);
105      this.active = recorder != NoOpMetricsRecorder.INSTANCE || tracer != NoOpTracerHook.INSTANCE;
106   }
107
108   /**
109    * Starts a new observation.
110    *
111    * <p>
112    * When both backends are the no-op singletons this returns {@link Observation#NOOP} without taking a
113    * timestamp or opening a span. Otherwise it opens a span via {@link TracerHook#startSpan(String)} and
114    * captures a start timestamp; the paired timer sample is recorded on {@link Observation#close()}.
115    *
116    * @param name The observation name &mdash; used as both the span name and the metric timer name.
117    *    Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
118    * @param tags Additional metric tags as comma-separated {@code key=value} pairs (e.g.
119    *    {@code "team=payments,region=us-east"}), or <jk>null</jk> / empty for none.
120    * @return The started {@link Observation}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
121    */
122   public Observation start(String name, String tags) {
123      assertArgNotNullOrBlank(ARG_name, name);
124      if (! active)
125         return Observation.NOOP;
126      var scope = tracer.startSpan(name);
127      return new ActiveObservation(recorder, scope, name, tags == null ? "" : tags);
128   }
129
130   /**
131    * Returns whether this observer has at least one live backend (metrics or tracing).
132    *
133    * @return <jk>true</jk> if either the metrics recorder or the tracer is non-no-op.
134    */
135   public boolean isActive() {
136      return active;
137   }
138
139   private static final class ActiveObservation implements Observation {
140
141      private final MetricsRecorder recorder;
142      private final Scope scope;
143      private final String name;
144      private final String tags;
145      private final long startNanos;
146      private Throwable error;
147
148      ActiveObservation(MetricsRecorder recorder, Scope scope, String name, String tags) {
149         this.recorder = recorder;
150         this.scope = scope;
151         this.name = name;
152         this.tags = tags;
153         this.startNanos = System.nanoTime();
154      }
155
156      @Override /* Observation */
157      public void setError(Throwable error) {
158         this.error = error;
159      }
160
161      @Override /* Observation */
162      public void close() {
163         var elapsed = Duration.ofNanos(System.nanoTime() - startNanos);
164         try {
165            if (nn(error))
166               scope.setError(error);
167         } finally {
168            try {
169               scope.close();
170            } finally {
171               recorder.record(name, tags, elapsed, error);
172            }
173         }
174      }
175   }
176}
